name: ctxctl
description: Restrict the top-level (orchestrator) thread to a small allowlist of orchestration tools, forcing real work into subagents so the main context stays clean. Subagents are never restricted.

allowlist.title: Top-level tool allowlist
allowlist.description: Comma-separated tool names the top-level thread may still call (no spaces). Everything else is blocked at the top level and must run inside a subagent. Subagents are never restricted. Read/Grep/Glob are included by default so the orchestrator can inspect code to plan; bulk reads should still go to a subagent. If a tool you need gets blocked, the denial message names it exactly so you can add it here.

mode.title: Block mode
mode.description: What to do when the top-level thread calls a non-allowlisted tool: 'deny' to block it outright, or 'ask' to prompt you for confirmation each time.

summary.title: Subagent summary contract
summary.description: Text injected into every subagent at spawn (via the SubagentStart hook) telling it how to write its final handoff. Leave blank to use the built-in contract, which asks for outcome, files touched, discrete findings (including incidental ones), and loose ends.
